trade.summary: Substantial bilateral trade under a Canada-Korea FTA (since 2015). Canada exports LNG, coal, uranium, canola, and copper to South Korea. South Korea exports vehicles (Hyundai, Kia), electronics (Samsung, LG), ships, and machinery to Canada.

diplomatic.summary: Strong bilateral relations deepened by the Korea-Canada FTA and shared security interests in the Indo-Pacific. Both are committed to the US-led alliance system and share concerns about North Korea and China. Active diplomatic calendar and growing strategic dialogue.

military.summary: Growing defense cooperation. Canada participates in Korean peninsula security discussions. Canadian troops participated in Korean War (significant historical bond). Joint exercises in Indo-Pacific. Growing defense industrial cooperation.

financial.summary: Growing bilateral investment. Korean EV battery makers (Samsung SDI, LG Energy Solution, SK On) are investing massively in Canadian battery manufacturing to access USMCA markets. Canadian pension funds invest in Korean assets.

energy.summary: South Korea is a major buyer of Canadian LNG and coal. Korean companies are investing in Canadian LNG infrastructure. Canada sees South Korea as a key energy partner in Asia. The energy relationship is strategically important for both countries.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation. Collaboration in nuclear energy (CANDU expertise, SMRs), battery technology, and semiconductor research. Canadian and Korean universities have exchange programs.
